label: Microsoft Outlook Mail
description: Arcade.dev LLM tools for Outlook Mail

tools.name: CreateAndSendEmail
tools.description: Create and immediately send a new email in Outlook to the specified recipients

parameters.name: subject
parameters.description: The subject of the email to create

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: The body of the email to create

parameters.name: to_recipients
parameters.description: The email addresses that will be the recipients of the email

parameters.name: cc_recipients
parameters.description: The email addresses that will be the CC recipients of the email.

parameters.name: bcc_recipients
parameters.description: The email addresses that will be the BCC recipients of the email.

parameters.name: body_type
parameters.description: The content type of the email body. Defaults to 'text'

output.description: A dictionary containing the created email details

tools.name: CreateDraftEmail
tools.description: Compose a new draft email in Outlook

parameters.name: subject
parameters.description: The subject of the draft email to create

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: The body of the draft email to create

parameters.name: to_recipients
parameters.description: The email addresses that will be the recipients of the draft email

parameters.name: cc_recipients
parameters.description: The email addresses that will be the CC recipients of the draft email.

parameters.name: bcc_recipients
parameters.description: The email addresses that will be the BCC recipients of the draft email.

parameters.name: body_type
parameters.description: The content type of the email body. Defaults to 'text'

output.description: A dictionary containing the created email details

tools.name: GetEmail
tools.description:
Retrieve a single email message by its ID.

Returns email metadata and body content. By default, the body is returned
as plain text (HTML tags stripped) and capped at 5000 characters. Set
body_format to HTML to get the original markup. Use body_offset to
continue reading long emails, or set max_body_characters to None for the
full body.

Use this tool to read the full content of an email after finding it via
search_emails or any listing tool.

parameters.name: message_id
parameters.description: The ID of the email message to retrieve.

parameters.name: body_format
parameters.description: Format for the returned email body. PLAIN_TEXT strips HTML tags for clean reading. HTML returns the original markup. Defaults to PLAIN_TEXT.

parameters.name: max_body_characters
parameters.description: Maximum number of characters to return from the email body. Defaults to 5000. Set to None to return the full body without a cap.

parameters.name: body_offset
parameters.description: Character offset to start reading the body from. Use this to continue reading a long email body that was previously capped. Defaults to 0 (start of body).

output.description: A dictionary containing the email message.

tools.name: ListEmailAttachments
tools.description:
List attachment metadata for an email message.

Returns metadata only (name, size, type, etc.). Attachment content is not included.
Use this tool when the user wants to know what files are attached to an email.

parameters.name: message_id
parameters.description: The ID of the email message to list attachments for.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of attachments to return. Max is 100. Defaults to 25.

parameters.name: pagination_token
parameters.description: The pagination token to continue a previous request

output.description: A dictionary containing a list of attachment metadata objects.

tools.name: ListEmails
tools.description:
List emails in the user's mailbox across all folders.

Results are sorted by sort_by in the sort_order direction.
Defaults to newest first (receivedDateTime descending).

Since this tool lists email across all folders, it may return sent items,
drafts, and other items that are not in the inbox.

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Property to sort results by. Defaults to received date.

parameters.name: sort_order
parameters.description: Sort direction. Defaults to descending.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The number of messages to return. Max is 100. Defaults to 10.

parameters.name: pagination_token
parameters.description: The pagination token to continue a previous request

output.description: A dictionary containing a list of emails

tools.name: ListEmailsByProperty
tools.description:
List emails in the user's mailbox across all folders filtering by a property.

Results are sorted by sort_by in the sort_order direction.
Defaults to newest first (receivedDateTime descending).

Sorting by RECEIVED_DATE_TIME works with any filter property. Sorting by
a different property (SUBJECT, SENDER, IMPORTANCE) while filtering by an
unrelated property may fail due to a Microsoft Graph API restriction. If
this happens, either sort by RECEIVED_DATE_TIME, or use list_emails (no
filter) with the desired sort_by.

parameters.name: mail_property
parameters.description: The property to filter the emails by.

parameters.name: operator
parameters.description: The operator to use for the filter.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The value to filter the emails by

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Property to sort results by. Defaults to received date. Sorting by received date works with any filter property. Sorting by a different property while filtering by another may not be supported — if the combination fails, use sort_by=RECEIVED_DATE_TIME or use list_emails with the desired sort_by instead.

parameters.name: sort_order
parameters.description: Sort direction. Defaults to descending.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The number of messages to return. Max is 100. Defaults to 10.

parameters.name: pagination_token
parameters.description: The pagination token to continue a previous request

output.description: A dictionary containing a list of emails

tools.name: ListEmailsInFolder
tools.description:
List the user's emails in the specified folder.

Exactly one of `well_known_folder_name` or `folder_id` MUST be provided.

Results are sorted by sort_by in the sort_order direction.
Defaults to newest first (receivedDateTime descending).

parameters.name: well_known_folder_name
parameters.description: The name of the folder to list emails from. Defaults to None.

parameters.name: folder_id
parameters.description: The ID of the folder to list emails from if the folder is not a well-known folder. Defaults to None.

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Property to sort results by. Defaults to received date.

parameters.name: sort_order
parameters.description: Sort direction. Defaults to descending.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The number of messages to return. Max is 100. Defaults to 10.

parameters.name: pagination_token
parameters.description: The pagination token to continue a previous request

output.description: A dictionary containing a list of emails and a pagination token, if applicable

tools.name: ListMailFolders
tools.description:
List mail folders in the user's mailbox.

Returns folder names, IDs, unread counts, and total item counts.
Use the folder ID in follow-up calls to list_emails_in_folder.
Omit parent_folder_id to list top-level folders, or provide a folder ID
to list its child folders.

parameters.name: parent_folder_id
parameters.description: ID of a parent folder to list child folders of. When omitted, lists top-level mailbox folders.

parameters.name: include_hidden
parameters.description: Whether to include hidden folders in the response. Defaults to False.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of folders to return. Max is 100. Defaults to 25.

parameters.name: pagination_token
parameters.description: The pagination token to continue a previous request.

output.description: A dictionary containing a list of mail folders.

tools.name: ReplyToEmail
tools.description:
Reply to an existing email in Outlook.

Use this tool to reply to the sender or all recipients of the email.
Specify the reply_type to determine the scope of the reply.

parameters.name: message_id
parameters.description: The ID of the email to reply to

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: The body of the reply to the email

parameters.name: reply_type
parameters.description: Specify ReplyType.REPLY to reply only to the sender or ReplyType.REPLY_ALL to reply to all recipients. Defaults to ReplyType.REPLY.

output.description: A dictionary containing the sent email details

tools.name: SearchEmails
tools.description:
Search emails across the user's entire mailbox.

Combines full-text keyword search with structured filters for sender,
read status, attachments, importance, and more. All provided parameters
are combined with AND. Results are ordered by date sent (most recent first).

Email bodies are truncated to 255 characters for efficient skimming.
Use get_email with the message_id to retrieve the full email content.

Use this tool when the user wants to find emails by content, topic, sender,
or a combination of criteria. For exact property filtering (e.g., by
conversationId or flag status), use list_emails_by_property instead.

parameters.name: keywords
parameters.description: Words or phrases to search for across email subject, body, and sender fields. All terms must match (AND operator). Defaults to None.

parameters.name: sender
parameters.description: Filter by sender email address, display name, or alias. When multiple values are provided, emails from any of the senders are returned (OR operator). Defaults to None.

parameters.name: recipient
parameters.description: Filter by To-recipient email address or display name. When multiple values are provided, emails to any of the recipients are returned (OR operator). Defaults to None.

parameters.name: attachment_name
parameters.description: Filter by attachment filename. When multiple values are provided, emails with any of the attachment names are returned (OR operator). Defaults to None.

parameters.name: has_attachments
parameters.description: Filter for emails with or without attachments. Defaults to None (no filter).

parameters.name: importance
parameters.description: Filter by email importance level. Defaults to None (no filter).

parameters.name: is_read
parameters.description: Filter by read status. True for read emails, False for unread. Defaults to None (no filter).

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Filter by email category name. Categories are user-defined. Defaults to None (no filter).

parameters.name: received_after
parameters.description: Filter for emails received on or after this date (YYYY-MM-DD). Dates are interpreted in UTC. Defaults to None (no filter).

parameters.name: received_before
parameters.description: Filter for emails received on or before this date (YYYY-MM-DD). Dates are interpreted in UTC. Defaults to None (no filter).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of search results to return. Max is 25. Defaults to 10.

parameters.name: pagination_token
parameters.description: The pagination token to continue a previous request.

output.description: A dictionary containing a list of matching emails.

tools.name: SendDraftEmail
tools.description:
Send an existing draft email in Outlook

This tool can send any un-sent email:
    - draft
    - reply-draft
    - reply-all draft
    - forward draft

parameters.name: message_id
parameters.description: The ID of the draft email to send

output.description: A dictionary containing the sent email details

tools.name: UpdateDraftEmail
tools.description:
Update an existing draft email in Outlook.

This tool overwrites the subject and body of a draft email (if provided),
and modifies its recipient lists by selectively adding or removing email addresses.

This tool can update any un-sent email:
    - draft
    - reply-draft
    - reply-all draft
    - forward draft

parameters.name: message_id
parameters.description: The ID of the draft email to update

parameters.name: subject
parameters.description: The new subject of the draft email. If provided, the existing subject will be overwritten

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: The new body of the draft email. If provided, the existing body will be overwritten

parameters.name: to_add
parameters.description: Email addresses to add as 'To' recipients.

parameters.name: to_remove
parameters.description: Email addresses to remove from the current 'To' recipients.

parameters.name: cc_add
parameters.description: Email addresses to add as 'CC' recipients.

parameters.name: cc_remove
parameters.description: Email addresses to remove from the current 'CC' recipients.

parameters.name: bcc_add
parameters.description: Email addresses to add as 'BCC' recipients.

parameters.name: bcc_remove
parameters.description: Email addresses to remove from the current 'BCC' recipients.

output.description: A dictionary containing the updated email details

tools.name: WhoAmI
tools.description:
Get comprehensive user profile and Outlook Mail environment information.

This tool provides detailed information about the authenticated user including
their name, email, mailbox settings, automatic replies configuration, and other
important profile details from Outlook Mail services.

output.description: Get comprehensive user profile and Outlook Mail environment information.
